description_long: This is a primitive-looking axe with a roughly hewn wooden handle and a single, pointed stone blade. The handle is long, slightly curved, and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, showing natural grain and some irregularities. The blade is a grayish stone, lashed securely to the handle with a thick cord or sinew, wrapped multiple times for stability. The head of the axe is asymmetrical, with the stone protruding more on one side, giving it a pick-like appearance. There are no visible metal parts or modern fasteners, and the overall construction suggests a handmade or early historical tool. The surface of both the handle and blade appears weathered and unpolished, with a matte, rough finish.
description: A primitive axe with a wooden handle and a stone blade, bound together with cord. The tool has a rough, handmade appearance and a matte, weathered finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the stone blade lashed to the top with cord.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile of the stone blade and the thickness of the cord wrapping.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the protruding stone blade and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the handle and the cord securing the blade, with minimal blade visibility.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural, irregular shape and rough texture; oval cross-section with no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single pointed stone blade, asymmetrical with a pick-like profile, no beard or poll, and a rough, unpolished edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional; blade is lashed to the handle with thick cord or sinew, no modern fasteners.
